textPlain: How is this a spacecraft? It can't ever leave orbit of a planet with an atmosphere.

This is Editorialised headline. Headline should beRedwire Awarded $44 Million DARPA Contract to Advance Very Low-Earth Orbit MissionEdit: And for those who are downvoting, it is actually in the guideline for submission. Otherwise please use the original title, unless it is misleading or linkbait; don't editorialize.
To those wondering, this is about using the thin air of near space to maintain orbit, not Skylon/SABRE-style air-breathing launch vehicle.
Neither could the space shuttle but surely that's a spacecraft.  Why would it have to leave a planet to count?
Most spacecraft we know of never left the Earth orbit anyway.
